title: DORA: Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR)
description: DORA Pillar: Mean Time to Recovery — Measures how long it takes to restore service after a production failure. Lower recovery times indicate strong monitoring, automated rollbacks, and effective incident response. Includes incident resolution time as a supplementary measure.

list.name: environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.label: Environment

list.name: copilot_cohort
list.query: All, Copilot Active, Copilot Inactive, Agent Users, Non-Agent Users
list.label: Copilot Cohort

list.name: Deployments
list.rawQuery:
SELECT d.created_at AS time, 'Deploy: ' || d.sha AS text, 'Deployments' AS tags
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

list.name: Incidents
list.rawQuery:
SELECT i.created_at AS time, 'Incident: ' || i.title AS text, 'Incidents' AS tags
FROM issues i
WHERE i.labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
  AND i.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

options.content:
# 🔧 Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR)

**DORA Pillar: Stability**

## What it Measures
Mean Time to Recovery measures how long it takes to restore service after a production failure or incident. It is the second DORA *stability* metric and reflects your team's ability to detect, diagnose, and resolve production issues quickly.

## Why it Matters
Fast recovery reduces the impact of failures on users and the business. Teams with low MTTR can deploy with confidence, knowing that any issues will be resolved quickly. High MTTR often indicates gaps in monitoring, incident response processes, or deployment automation.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Recovery Time | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | Less than 1 hour | Automated rollbacks, strong monitoring, fast incident response |
| 🟡 High | 1 hour to 1 day | Good processes with some manual intervention |
| 🟠 Medium | 1 day to 1 week | Slow diagnosis or complex recovery procedures |
| 🔴 Low | More than 1 week | Major gaps in monitoring or incident response |

**Incident Resolution Time** tracks issue-based incidents as a supplementary measure.

## How to Take Action
- **If high MTTR**: Implement automated rollback capabilities, improve monitoring and alerting, create runbooks for common failures
- **If incident resolution is slow**: Review incident response processes, ensure on-call coverage, reduce time-to-detection with better alerts
- **If trending up**: Look for increasing system complexity, new failure modes, or reduced team expertise
- **If consistently low**: Your team has strong operational maturity — consider sharing practices as a model for others

panels.title: 🔧 Recovery Time (MTTR)
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Median time to recover from a failed deployment

🟢 Elite: <1 hr  ·  🟡 High: <1 day  ·  🟠 Medium: <1 week  ·  🔴 Low: >1 week

targets.rawSql:
WITH failed_deployments AS (
  SELECT d.id AS deployment_id, d.environment, ds.created_at AS failure_time
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
  WHERE ds.state IN ('failure', 'error')
    AND d.environment = '$environment'
    AND ds.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
recovery AS (
  SELECT fd.deployment_id, fd.failure_time,
    MIN(ds2.created_at) AS recovery_time
  FROM failed_deployments fd
  JOIN deployments d2 ON d2.environment = fd.environment AND d2.created_at > fd.failure_time
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds2 ON ds2.deployment_id = d2.id AND ds2.state = 'success'
  WHERE ds2.created_at > fd.failure_time
  GROUP BY fd.deployment_id, fd.failure_time
)
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovery_time - failure_time)) / 3600
) AS median_recovery_hours
FROM recovery

panels.title: 🚨 Incident Resolution Time
panels.description:
Supplementary · Median time to resolve incidents labeled "incident"

🟢 Fast: <4 hrs  ·  🟡 Normal: <12 hrs  ·  🟠 Slow: <48 hrs  ·  🔴 Very Slow: >48 hrs

targets.rawSql:
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (i.closed_at - i.created_at)) / 3600
) AS median_resolution_hours
FROM issues i
WHERE i.labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
  AND i.closed_at IS NOT NULL
  AND i.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: 📉 Recovery Time Trend
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Weekly median time to recover from failed deployments
Lower is better — Elite teams recover in under 1 hour

targets.rawSql:
WITH failed_deployments AS (
  SELECT d.id AS deployment_id, d.environment, ds.created_at AS failure_time
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
  WHERE ds.state IN ('failure', 'error')
    AND d.environment = '$environment'
    AND ds.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
recovery AS (
  SELECT fd.deployment_id, fd.failure_time,
    MIN(ds2.created_at) AS recovery_time
  FROM failed_deployments fd
  JOIN deployments d2 ON d2.environment = fd.environment AND d2.created_at > fd.failure_time
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds2 ON ds2.deployment_id = d2.id AND ds2.state = 'success'
  WHERE ds2.created_at > fd.failure_time
  GROUP BY fd.deployment_id, fd.failure_time
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', failure_time) AS time,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovery_time - failure_time)) / 3600
  ) AS median_recovery_hours
FROM recovery
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

SELECT i.number, i.title, u.login AS assignee, i.created_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - i.created_at)) / 3600 AS hours_open
FROM issues i
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = i.assignee_user_id
WHERE i.labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
  AND i.state = 'open'
  AND i.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY i.created_at DESC
